skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem:
The frequency distribution below summarizes a set of data, where n is a positive integer.

Value 1: frequency n

Value 2: frequency n+2

Value 3: frequency n+5

Value 4: frequency n+2

Value 5: frequency n

How much greater is the mean of the set of data than the median?
rationale: The frequencies of the values below 3 (values 1 and 2) match the frequencies of the values above 3 (values 4 and 5). The distribution is symmetric about the value 3. In a symmetric distribution the mean and median are equal, so their difference is 0.
